description:
Given the app header is rendered and the app switcher icon-button is visible
When the user hovers over the app switcher icon-button
Then a tooltip labelled "Switch apps" appears anchored to the icon-button

Given the app switcher is in its default (closed) state
When the user clicks the app switcher icon-button
Then the panel opens anchored to the trigger with a 4px gap
And the icon-button enters selected state
And all other open header overlays are closed

scenario: Panel shows accessible apps with icon + label
description:
Given the app switcher panel is open
When the panel renders
Then all applications accessible to the authenticated user are displayed
And each app shows its product icon and text label

Given the app switcher panel is open
When the panel renders
Then no applications the user cannot access are displayed

Given the app switcher panel is open and the app list exceeds the panel height
When the number of applications overflows
Then the panel scrolls vertically to reveal all items
And no applications are permanently clipped

scenario: Selecting an app navigates to its login page
description:
Given the app switcher panel is open
When the user selects an application
Then the user is navigated to that application's login page (SSO not implemented)
And the panel closes on navigation

Given the app switcher panel is open
When the user clicks anywhere outside the panel
Then the panel closes
And the icon-button returns to its default (non-selected) state

scenario: ESC closes panel and returns focus
description:
Given the app switcher panel is open
When the user presses the ESC key
Then the panel closes
And keyboard focus returns to the app switcher trigger

Given the app switcher panel is open
When the user activates another header overlay (account switcher, avatar, search, or help)
Then the app switcher panel closes
And the newly triggered overlay opens
And only one overlay is open across the header

Given the user is on a mobile viewport
When the user taps the hamburger (bars) icon
Then a full-screen panel opens with primary nav items and a "Switch application" row
When the user taps "Switch application"
Then the accessible application list is revealed with icons and labels
When the user selects an application
Then the user is navigated to that application's login page and the panel closes

Given the user is on a tablet viewport
When the user taps the hamburger (bars) icon
Then a 320px fixed-width panel opens with primary nav items and a "Switch application" row
When the user taps "Switch application"
Then the accessible application list is revealed
And if content exceeds the panel height the list scrolls

Given the app header is loaded and the user navigates via keyboard
When a keyboard user tabs to the app switcher icon-button
Then a visible WCAG 2.2 AA focus ring is displayed
And the icon-button is activatable via Enter and Space keys

Given the app switcher panel is open and the user navigates via keyboard
When the user presses the arrow keys
Then focus moves between application items
When the user presses Enter
Then the focused application item is selected

Given the user is unauthenticated or their session has expired
When the user attempts to open the app switcher
Then the user is redirected to the NowInfinity login page
And the app switcher panel does not open

Given the app switcher panel is open
When the user selects an application
Then a Pendo analytics event is recorded for the app switch click

Given the app header is rendered
Then the app switcher uses the sl-app-switcher dark variant from the HUB24 Design System

scenario: Second click toggles the panel closed
description:
Given the app switcher panel is open and the icon-button is selected
When the user clicks the app switcher icon-button again
Then the panel closes and the icon-button returns to default state (toggle behaviour to confirm)

scenario: Selecting an app the user is already active in
description:
Given the app switcher panel is open
When the user selects an application they are already active in
Then the user is routed to that application's home page (Q&A agreed; spec says login page — decision pending)

Given the app switcher panel is open
Then an optional "What is [App]?" info link may appear beside each app (Release 1 scope undecided — UX to confirm)

Given the app switcher is opened and the product list cannot be loaded
When the fetch fails
Then an appropriate error state/message is shown (relevance pending — apps may be known at session time)
